title: Reverse Proxies, Forwarded Fields, Framing, and Request Smuggling Defense · Type-System Trace: Message Framing Ambiguity
summary: The origin and proxy disagree about framing, forwarded fields are appended without trust, hop-by-hop fields cross boundaries, and parser tolerance is enabled to fix malformed clients. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts message framing ambiguity increment in proxy hop, framing, and parser-consistency contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject conflicting Content-Length, transfer-coding ambiguity, invalid field syntax, premature EOF, excess body, and front-end or origin disagreement.
Require trusted proxies to overwrite client-controlled Forwarded and X-Forwarded values and validate the observed hop count against every reachable topology.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Message Framing Ambiguity · Theory Message Framing Ambiguity
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 7915 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reject untrusted topology, ambiguous framing, and insecure parser policy; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then abort during a dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Message Framing Ambiguity contract for the supplied data.
